name: gitbash-clipboard-cd
description: Windows Git Bash helpers (cdc / cdh) to cd into folder paths copied from Explorer — fixes backslash-path mangling and reads clipboard history.

methods.name: shell-hook
methods.command: echo gitbash-clipboard-cd: cdc/cdh functions and the cdh-cliphist.ps1 helper are written by the per-group shell-alias hook in install.sh

install.notes: Windows-only, no external software and no repo payload. The cdc/cdh bash functions AND the cdh clipboard-history helper (~/.local/share/gitbash-clipboard-cd/cdh-cliphist.ps1) are both written by the per-group shell-alias hook (install_gitbash_clipboard_cd_aliases in install.sh, Step 8), matching how claude-code-sessions inlines its cs() wrapper. The smoke test runs before Step 8, so it shows one cosmetic warning on a clean first install, then passes. Avoid double quotes in check/command — the installer's field extractor truncates at the first quote.

post_install_hints: Open a new Git Bash window (or run: source ~/.bashrc) to load cdc / cdh.
cdh requires Windows Clipboard History — enable it at Settings -> System -> Clipboard, or press Win+V and turn it on.
Usage: copy a folder in Explorer (or right-click -> Copy as path), then type cdc (current clipboard) or cdh (most recent directory in clipboard history).
